revision.comment: removed [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] using [[WP:HC|HotCat]] Repetitive
you must let the CFR process play out, and may not preemptively move pages into the redlink in advance of the category actually being moved

Bumping for adding the Kofun template
fixing error that broke all the categories

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Koumorizuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_09.html Sōja city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/667850.html Okaya Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji ware]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji pottery]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji pottery]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōmorizuka Kofun | native_name =こうもり塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koumoridzuka kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōmorizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|3.35|N|133|47|11.79|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kōmorizuka Kofun'''|こうもり塚古墳|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kanbayashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162118|title=こうもり塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It was formerly known as the {{nihongo|'''Kurohimezuka Kofun'''|黒姫塚古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Kōmorizuka Kofun is located on a hill in-between the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji]] temple ruins and the [[Bitchū Kokubun-ji#Bitchū Kokubun-niji|Bitchū Kokubun-niji]] nunnery ruins. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is estimated that the tumulus was about 100 meters long and had a posterior circular portion with a diameter of about 55 to 60 meters. The tumulus is constructed in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[haniwa]]'' have not been confirmed. On the southern side of the posterior portion is a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] constructed of megalithic blocks of [[granite]]. The burial chamber has a total length of about 19.4 meters, which is the third largest size among the confirmed corridor-style stone chambers in Japan. It contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.38m long, 1.4m wide, and 1.31m high. The sarcophagus is made of shell [[limestone]] quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]].The 1978 [[archaeological excavation]] found fragments of a tortoise shell-shaped [[Haji pottery]] coffin and iron nails, indicating that the stone sarcophagus contained both a pottery and a wooden coffin, possibly from multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although the tumulus had been robbed in antiquity, some [[grave goods]] were discovered, including pommels of iron swords with a phoenix design, iron arrowheads, horse trapping and ornaments, such as small glass beads and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is named "Komorizuka" from bats living in the open burial chamber. The name "Kurohimezuka" comes from a legend that this is the tomb of Kurohime, a beautiful woman from the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] who was favored by [[Emperor Nintoku]]. However, as the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century, and the reign of Emperor Nintoku is said to be in the 4th century, the dates do not match the legend.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Koumorizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Panoramic view File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 05.jpg|Anterior rectangular portion File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 07.jpg|Posterior circular portion File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Entry File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Passage File:Koumorizuka Kofun, sekishitsu-3.jpg|Inside the burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koumorizuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_09.html Sōja city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/667850.html Okaya Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
